intro.title: Deus Vult
intro.body: Urban II preached the Crusade at Clermont in November 1095. The princely armies — Bohemond, Godfrey, Raymond — converged on Constantinople in 1096-97, crossed Anatolia, took Antioch by siege, and stormed Jerusalem in July 1099. The Gesta Francorum is the eyewitness.

stops.name: Constantinople
stops.note: Crusader armies muster outside the walls 1096-97. Alexios extracts oaths of fealty before ferrying them across the Bosphorus.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Latin knights camped outside Theodosian walls, Greek galleys ferrying horses across the Bosphorus.

stops.name: Nicaea
stops.note: Captured 19 June 1097. The Seljuk capital falls jointly to Crusader siege and Byzantine fleet on Lake Ascanius.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Lake-side city under siege from land, Greek galleys on the water, Latin towers on the shore.

stops.name: Dorylaeum
stops.note: 1 July 1097. Bohemond's vanguard absorbs Kilij Arslan's attack at dawn; the main army arrives at midday and wins (Gesta IX).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Anatolian plain at dawn, Latin knights forming a defensive ring against Turkish horse archers.

stops.name: Antioch
stops.note: Seven-month siege October 1097 - June 1098. Bohemond enters through Firouz's tower; Kerbogha's relief army defeated 28 June.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Antioch citadel above the besieging camp, scaling ladder against the tower, dawn ascent.

stops.name: Jerusalem
stops.note: Stormed 15 July 1099. Godfrey's tower mounts the northern wall first; the city falls amid massacre. Godfrey becomes Advocatus.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Holy City walls with siege tower against the northern stretch, banners on the parapet.
